reason: The leaked RST directive `:doc:`./UnpublishedApi`` appears only in the final sentence and does not distort any factual content. An AI agent reading the sentence "仕様および使用方法は :doc:`./UnpublishedApi` を参照。" will encounter unexpected punctuation/syntax but can still infer that a reference document for UnpublishedApi exists. All substantive information (tool names, purposes, warnings, configuration guidance) is intact and correctly represented. The defect is cosmetic noise, not a factual error or omission that would cause the agent to give wrong guidance.

findings_assessment.justification: The RST directive `:doc:`./UnpublishedApi`` is rendered as literal punctuation/text, which is syntactically odd but does not mislead the agent. The sentence still communicates that a specification/usage document for UnpublishedApi exists. No factual information is lost or corrupted. The fix did not apply in Phase E: the Phase E output is byte-for-byte identical to the Phase D finding — the fix agent ran but made no change to the file, leaving the directive in place.
